mode_selected: Mode 3 -> Mode 4 escalation (cost-engineering audit surfaced a Mode 4 PROP candidate)
mode_selection_reason: No pending operator directives. No Mode 0 assigned-to-tinker items. Mode 1 GREEN (all agents fresh, no stranded patches). Mode 2 GREEN-with-disk-warning (FUSE matches GitHub on 5 checked files, auth 200, disk 690MB project-relative — within expected workspace+clone footprint, no leak). Backlog healthy (52 open, 0 aged >=14d, net velocity +111/7d). Verified PROP-066 / PROP-070 / PROP-060 / PROP-059 all FIXED. Ran Mode 3 cost-engineering pass; surfaced a serious recurrence finding (PROP-066 silent-skip) that justified inline Mode 4 PROP authoring.

Verified PROP-066 helper script working via manual invocation (wrote sync-workspace-runs-2026-05-31T16-52-42Z.json correctly). mode_4_proposals_self_fixes.evidence: Authored PROP-072 status=design-pending-operator-review. No self-apply (prompt files require operator approval per recent pattern). PROP-070 canaries (a) decider Step A0c sweep mention and (c) ISS-2311/2337/2365 auto-close are still pending (decider has not run since PROP-070 deploy).

findings.title: PROP-066 (Step 4c helper) silent-skip RECURRING after PROP-068 collapsed-block fix

evidence_summary.tinker_manual_invocation_test_at_16_52Z: PASSED — wrote sync-workspace-runs-2026-05-31T16-52-42Z.json; classification=equal, upstream_newer_than_last_sync=true, need_sync=true, action=pull-moved-auto-sync, files_copied=N, sync_exit_code=0
evidence_summary.second_source_confirmation: integrity-2026-05-31T16-00 latest summary's FUSE-staleness finding says 'PROP-066 Step 4c should self-heal on next workspace-sync cycle' — implies integrity has also observed Step 4c is not running on the cycles it expects.

findings.root_cause_hypothesis: LLM agent's execution pattern is robust at FIRST + LAST positions within a single bash block (4a commit at top, 4b run-report + cleanup at bottom both reliably run) but unreliable at MIDDLE positions (Step 4c invocation in the middle is dropped). PROP-068's structural collapse removed one form of skip-boundary (trailing-section-header) but did not pin agent execution to ALL lines within the collapsed block.
findings.recommended_action: Operator review + approve PROP-072 primary fix (reorder Step 4c to FIRST position after trap, before Step 4a). Secondary backstop (AGENT_NOTES self-attestation) and tertiary startup-check are defense-in-depth options.
findings.priority: high (defects in git->FUSE propagation cause FUSE-staleness which causes downstream agents to read stale data; integrity report already flagged one such case this cycle)

findings.title: Project footprint 690MB triggers Mode 2 WARNING threshold but is within expected operating range
findings.evidence_summary: FUSE workspace 380MB + own tinker clone 299MB + 1MB scratch = 680MB. No leaked clones. Root partition at 96% used is dominated by Ubuntu system image (~8GB), not project artifacts. The 500MB threshold is calibrated for leak detection; the expected operating footprint without leaks is 600-700MB.
findings.recommended_action: Consider raising Mode 2 disk-WARNING threshold from 500MB to 800MB so it doesn't flap on healthy operation. Will revisit if it persists across multiple runs.
